evals.prompt: I get 20-30 signups a week for my AI pitch-deck tool but almost nobody creates a deck. They sign up, click around, and leave. Sign-up is email+password, then they land on an empty dashboard. Help.
evals.expected_output: A defined activation event (first deck generated), an audit of the current flow flagging the empty dashboard as the core failure, a rebuilt ≤3-step flow to first deck in under 3 minutes, welcome + day-3 email drafts, empty-state copy, and activation tracking metrics.
assertions: A single specific activation event is defined with a trackable event name
The empty dashboard is identified as a primary friction point with a concrete replacement (guided first action or example data)
The recommended flow gets users to the core action in 3 or fewer steps
Both a welcome email and a day-3 non-activated follow-up email are drafted
Activation rate and time-to-activation metrics are defined with targets

evals.prompt: what should my activation metric be? my app lets teachers make interactive quizzes. students join with a code. teachers sign up, some make a quiz but never run it live with a class.
evals.expected_output: A reasoned activation definition — likely 'first quiz run live with real students' rather than 'quiz created', since value is experienced in the live session — with the tracking event, a target, and how to move creators to first live run.
assertions: The output reasons about where value is actually experienced rather than defaulting to the first obvious action
A single activation event is chosen and justified against the quiz-created alternative
A trackable implementation (event name or query) is specified
At least 2 concrete tactics are given to move quiz-creators to their first live session
